events.title: Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout
events.description: Three Indian seafarers were killed in an attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, as renewed hostilities in one of the world’s most critical shipping corridors once again heightened concern over food security, fuel prices and broken global supply chains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout

events.title: Violent attacks on schools, pupils and staff around the world up by 40%, says study
events.description: Cases reported in 83 countries, with at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted or arrested, GCPEA says Attacks on education globally have surged by 40% with more than 8,556 recorded incidents and 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, arrested or otherwise harmed in 2024 and 2025, according to new research. Attacks were reported in 83 countries, with the highest incidences recorded in Colombia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Haiti, Palestine and Ukraine. Continue reading...

events.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres
events.description: ‘Coastal uplift’ exposes coral and kills marine life, as residents say shorelines extended by up to 200 metres A powerful earthquake that killed at least 61 people in the Philippines this week raised the seabed by as much as 2 metres (6.6ft), exposing coral and harming marine life, the country’s environment department has said. At least 40 people are still missing after the 7.8-magnitude quake off southern Mindanao island on Monday, according to updated tolls from the disaster agency. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres

events.title: Rinehart buys ‘significant’ stake in Elon Musk’s SpaceX – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The prime minister and foreign minister have issued a lengthier statement welcoming the agreement made by the US and Iran, and called for continued restraint to avoid further escalation. President Donald Trump made the announcement he had signed a deal this morning, saying the strait of Hormuz would be reopened. Continued restraint and constructive engagement will be essential to prevent further escalation and secure a lasting agreement. We are pleased the agreement between the US and Iran includes steps to reopening the Strait of Hormuz … We encourage all parties to use this opportunity to pursue a durable and lasting peace through dialogue and diplomacy. Iran must address longstanding concerns about its nuclear program and the threat it poses to international security. Continue reading...

events.title: World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe
events.description: Violence spread across Belfast following a shocking knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese asylum seeker on Monday, triggering a wave of anti-immigration unrest. The victim suffered significant injuries to his face and back.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe

events.title: Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames
events.description: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was set on fire as Russia struck multiple cities in Ukraine overnight. Meanwhile, Moscow reported three deaths from a Ukrainian drone attack.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
events.description: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
events.description: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children

events.title: Relief from world leaders as US-Iran deal announced
events.description: The announcement that the United States and Iran will sign an agreement this Friday, has been met with relief from world leaders while, in a joint statement, the UK, France, Germany and Italy called for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the return of regional stability. They also reafirmed that preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons must remain a priority.

events.title: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
events.description: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

events.title: Partner of woman killed by dogs recently saved neighbor wounded in similar attack: court records
events.description: Florida woman Linda Cutler charged with manslaughter after her dogs allegedly mauled neighbor Jodi Cowan to death in a Brevard County attack.

events.title: Christian leaders hold emergency summit in Jerusalem to confront global rise in antisemitism
events.description: International Christian Embassy Jerusalem is hosting an emergency summit addressing rising antisemitism and replacement theology amid church leaders' silence.

events.title: Brisbane teenager allegedly plotted terror attacks on Liberal party targets, court hears
events.description: Teenager denies planning attacks on party figures and Labour Day march after allegedly being influenced by the Unabomber Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Brisbane private schoolboy allegedly plotted terrorist attacks against the Liberal party and Brisbane’s Labour Day march in 2024 after being influenced by the Unabomber. The teenager, who cannot be named for legal reasons, was 16 in 2024 when he was arrested by counter-terrorism police. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Brisbane teenager allegedly plotted terror attacks on Liberal party targets, court hears

events.title: Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct
events.description: Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct

events.title: Mixed emotions among diaspora as Iran begins World Cup campaign
events.description: Tonight, Iran begin their World Cup campaign against New Zealand in Los Angeles. It's a match taking place under unprecedented conditions. For the first time in World Cup history, a host nation is at war with one of its participating teams. That conflict is shaping everything around Iran's presence in the United States: from strict restrictions on where the team can be based, to divisions within the Iranian diaspora itself.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mixed emotions among diaspora as Iran begins World Cup campaign

events.title: World News in Brief: State-supported safe houses in Haiti, EU pact strengthens refugee protection, demand for ‘critical minerals’ intensifies
events.description: Haiti has opened its first State-supported safe house for survivors of sexual violence with support from the UN, marking a significant step in responding to the growing crisis affecting women and girls.

events.title: ‘Spy turtles’ and ‘spy fish’ being used to monitor Chinese waters, Beijing claims
events.description: Ministry says animals fitted with sensors by foreign agencies collect sensitive sea data, in ‘invisible secret war’ China’s ministry of state security has claimed that foreign espionage and intelligence agencies are using innovative new methods to monitor the country’s waters, including deploying “spy” animals fitted with sensors. In a post on the Chinese platform WeChat on Friday, the ministry warned that an “invisible secret war” was quietly playing out in the seas around China as foreign agencies were collecting sensitive data “through a variety of new spying devices” to produce underwater maps that pose a “serious threat to our national security”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Spy turtles’ and ‘spy fish’ being used to monitor Chinese waters, Beijing claims

events.title: U.S. and Iran announce a deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The deal is a major breakthrough in the conflict that set the Middle East aflame and shook the global economy. However, it did not resolve critical issues set aside for further negotiations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. and Iran announce a deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Iran take World Cup centre stage as they arrive in US
events.description: Iran's football team arrived in the United States for the first time at this World Cup on Sunday, landing at Los Angeles International Airport and holding a press conference on the same day that a peace deal between the two nations was announced. The Iran squad arrived after a short flight from Tijuana, Mexico, where they left their base ​camp earlier to a ‌rousing sendoff ahead of their opening game against New Zealand at Los Angeles Stadium on Monday.

events.title: G7: Leaders gather to grapple with wars, global economy
events.description: The heads of the world's richest nations gather in France as the wars in Iran and Ukraine dominate the global agenda. The US has promised peace in Iran; fellow leaders are cautiously optimistic. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 G7: Leaders gather to grapple with wars, global economy

events.title: Vast Eurosatory weapons showcase gets underway
events.description: The Eurosatory World Defense exhibition is taking place in Paris's Villepinte exhibition centre for the next five days. It is the world's largest defense and security trade fair, and this year it is bigger than ever, with over 2,500 exhibitors participating from over 65 countries, driven by a surge in global military spending amid escalating conflicts over the past two years

events.title: French fans flood New York ahead of world Cup opener
events.description: It is countdown time for the French national side's first World Cup match. Les Bleus are set to arrive in New York before facing Senegal on Tuesday. New York has been celebrating its NBA title after victory against the San Antonio Spurs. French fans who have already arrived in New York have been soaking up the festive atmosphere.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ French fans flood New York ahead of world Cup opener

events.title: Iran war: Will the global energy crisis end soon?
events.description: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Will the global energy crisis end soon?

events.title: What we know about the US-Iran memorandum of understanding
events.description: The United States and Iran, ⁠as well as mediator Pakistan, ​have provided few details about the preliminary deal announced Sunday to bring an end to the US-Iran conflict that has engulfed the region and threatened global energy supplies.

events.title: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
events.description: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears

events.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
events.description: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

events.title: ‘Life-changing’ injuries: family reels from ‘tragic situation’ as Coogee shark attack victim loses arm
events.description: The family of Leah Stewart is braced for more bad news and the impact on her 18-month-old daughter Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The family of the woman critically injured in Saturday’s horrific shark attack at Coogee beach is still coming to terms with the catastrophic scope of her injuries and the heartbreaking impact on her toddler. Leah Stewart, a 35-year-old primary school teacher, mother and dedicated ocean swimmer, remained on life support in the intensive care unit at St Vincent’s hospital after being mauled by a suspected 3.5-metre great white shark. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Life-changing’ injuries: family reels from ‘tragic situation’ as Coogee shark attack victim loses arm

events.title: Iran updates: World leaders express optimism for peace deal
events.description: World leaders have welcomed news of a peace deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. However, many details remain unconfirmed. DW has more.

events.title: ‘Human rights are part of our DNA’: UN launches global alliance to counter rising threats
events.description: The UN human rights office (OHCHR) has launched a Global Alliance for Human Rights, a broad coalition aimed at placing the issue at the heart of decision-making, when conflict levels have reached a record high amid deepening inequality and accelerating climate change.

events.title: UK Court of Appeal upholds ban on Palestine Action as ‘terrorist’ group
events.description: Judge says the group's behaviour was not that of a non-violent, direct action organisation.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK Court of Appeal upholds ban on Palestine Action as ‘terrorist’ group

events.title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
events.description: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin

events.title: Hate speech spreading ‘faster than ever’, warns UN chief as global action plan is revealed
events.description: Hate speech is a grave and growing threat to peace and security, Secretary-General António Guterres warned on Thursday, calling for urgent action to break a cycle that can lead to genocide and other atrocity crimes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hate speech spreading ‘faster than ever’, warns UN chief as global action plan is revealed

events.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
events.description: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze

events.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
events.description: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia , which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds

events.title: National Guardsman pleads guilty to fatal shooting of soldier he found in bed with his ex-girlfriend
events.description: A U.S. Army National Guardsman pleaded guilty to killing a soldier he found in bed with his ex-girlfriend at Fort Gordon in Augusta, Georgia.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 National Guardsman pleads guilty to fatal shooting of soldier he found in bed with his ex-girlfriend

events.title: After years of isolation, Myanmar works to reopen international relations
events.description: A visit to India and planned visit to China by Myanmar's leader signal a reopening of international relations for a country that has been isolated since the 2021 coup.

events.title: AI, drones and Ukrainian tech on show at Eurosatory defence fair
events.description: More than 2,500 exhibitors are taking part in the Eurosatory defence fair this week near Paris, one of the world's biggest weapon shows. James Andre, reporting from the show, says that AI, drones and weapons developed by Ukraine during its war with Russia are among the tech in the spotlight this year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI, drones and Ukrainian tech on show at Eurosatory defence fair

events.title: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict
events.description: UN Secretary General António Guterres welcomed on Sunday a new peace deal between the United States and Iran, calling it a “critical step” toward ending the conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict

events.title: Refugee numbers drop for first time in a decade, but millions remain trapped
events.description: Global forced displacement has decreased for the first time in a decade, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) reported on Thursday, though the figure remains unacceptably high and tens of millions of people are still trapped in prolonged exile with little prospect of rebuilding their lives.

events.title: Multiple people wounded in shooting at South Carolina's largest mall; authorities detain suspects
events.description: Multiple people were wounded in a shooting at Haywood Mall in Greenville, South Carolina, the largest mall in the state, police confirmed Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Multiple people wounded in shooting at South Carolina's largest mall; authorities detain suspects

events.title: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report
events.description: Mexican authorities found a decomposing corpse with signs of violence near Tijuana's Caliente Stadium, where Iran's team trains for the World Cup.

events.title: Swiss reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
events.description: Voters in Switzerland on Sunday narrowly rejected a divisive anti-immigration proposal to cap the country's population, which had sparked warnings of "chaos". Nearly 55 percent of Swiss voters rejected the initiative put forward by the hard-right Swiss People's Party (SVP), which sought to stop the Alpine nation's population -- currently 9.1 million -- going above 10 million before 2050.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Swiss reject proposal to cap population at 10 million

events.title: At least 31 dead after Ethiopia bus crash
events.description: At least ‌31 people have died and 33 others were injured after a bus ‌plunged into a ravine ​in Ethiopia, police said.
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 31 dead after Ethiopia bus crash

events.title: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
events.description: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal

events.title: Spain prodigy Lamine Yamal named UN Goodwill Ambassador on World Cup opening day
events.description: The biggest-ever football World Cup begins on Thursday, across three countries and two continents, with over 100 games. The UN is raising awareness of the game’s powerful capacity to serve as a platform for sustainable development and social justice.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Spain prodigy Lamine Yamal named UN Goodwill Ambassador on World Cup opening day

events.title: World welcomes US-Iran ‘peace deal’ criticised by Israel
events.description: Governments across the world have welcomed the tentative deal between the US and Iran to end the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World welcomes US-Iran ‘peace deal’ criticised by Israel

events.title: Ebola risk for World Cup is ‘extremely low’, but US is ready, experts say
events.description: US hospitals and agencies have enhanced preparedness for Ebola, but some experts warn of public health system strains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola risk for World Cup is ‘extremely low’, but US is ready, experts say

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't restore normal prewar shipping any time soon
events.description: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strait of Hormuz reopening won't restore normal prewar shipping any time soon

events.title: Oil prices drop and stocks enjoy cautious rally on US-Iran peace progress
events.description: There is cautious optimism on financial markets that a US-Iran peace deal will ease the hit to the global economy caused by their war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil prices drop and stocks enjoy cautious rally on US-Iran peace progress

events.title: 11 skydivers, one pilot killed in Missouri plane crash near airport
events.description: Authorities say at least 12 people were killed after an aircraft crashed near Butler Memorial Airport in Missouri, prompting a multi-agency investigation.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ 11 skydivers, one pilot killed in Missouri plane crash near airport

events.title: ‘Island surrounded by war’: Crimeans panic amid Ukrainian attacks
events.description: Fuel shortages and fears rock Crimea as Kyiv attacks the annexed peninsula.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Island surrounded by war’: Crimeans panic amid Ukrainian attacks

events.title: US musician Oliver Tree, 32, killed in helicopter crash in Brazil
events.description: Alternative singer and internet personality among six who died when two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro The American musician Oliver Tree has died in a helicopter crash in Brazil at the age of 32, according to reports. Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people onboard, including Tree, several Brazilian media outlets reported. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US musician Oliver Tree, 32, killed in helicopter crash in Brazil

events.title: Jessie J’s triumphant return puts lucrative Chinese market in spotlight
events.description: Other western acts have attempted to crack country’s music scene since singer’s breakout success in 2018 One week after announcing she was “cancer free”, the British pop star Jessie J did what any recovering patient would do and travelled thousands of miles around the world to perform for an audience of more than a billion people. On 29 May, the singer-songwriter, whose real name is Jessica Cornish, belted out a stage-rattling rendition of Frank Sinatra’s My Way on the stage of Singer, a hugely popular Chinese singing competition similar to The Voice. She also performed her new song, California, briefly adapting the lyrics to change California to Changsha, the Chinese city where Singer is hosted. Continue reading...

events.title: Mixed reactions in Lebanon and Israel over Iran-US ceasefire
events.description: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement Monday that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but significant challenges remain to ending the war, including whether Israel will continue its offensive in Lebanon. The deal was met by sceptism by some in Beirut who doubt if it will really bring an end to the destruction, while in Israel some would like to see the war continue.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mixed reactions in Lebanon and Israel over Iran-US ceasefire

events.title: G7 summit: 'Pressure will be on leaders to show support for Ukraine'
events.description: As G7 leaders meet in France this week, Iran and the peace deal signed with the US is likely to be in the spotlight. But with Volodymyr Zelensky in attendance, Ukraine is also set to be a major point of discussion, with leaders expected to show their support for the country in the wake of recent Russian attacks. France 24's Philip Turle has the latest.

events.title: 'Sudanese people have been fighting for freedom for decades': Director Hind Meddeb
events.description: The director of a film that shows how the people of Sudan have spent years fighting for their freedom has spoken of its power. Amid the civil war in Sudan that has killed tens of thousands since it broke out in 2023, a film made four years earlier during the 2019 uprising against the dictatorship has become something of a beacon for the Sudanese people. "Sudan, Remember Us" is being shown in Paris at a masterclass that discusses how difficult and important it is to film demonstrations and uprisings so that they can be show in the future. We spoke to director Hind Meddeb in Perspective.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Sudanese people have been fighting for freedom for decades': Director Hind Meddeb

events.title: Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic
events.description: In the heart of the Central African Republic (CAR), a nation long marked by conflict, Nina Mireille Yankinon stands as a symbol of resilience, leadership, and hope, dedicating her life to helping communities torn apart by war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic

events.title: Ukraine civilian casualty toll in May highest in four years, UN monitors say
events.description: More civilians were killed and injured in Ukraine in May than in any other month in the past four years, UN investigators said on Friday in their latest update.

events.title: WHO report shows progress in blood safety, but there are worrying gaps
events.description: Every day, safe blood helps save the lives of women experiencing childbirth complications, accident victims, cancer patients and people living with chronic diseases. Yet despite decades of progress, access to lifesaving blood remains deeply unequal, with shortages continuing to put lives at risk in many lower-income countries, according to a new World Health Organization (WHO) report.

events.title: Expert warns of 'general escalation' of fighting if Houthis resume Red Sea campaign
events.description: State Department calls Houthi Red Sea shipping ban unacceptable as experts warn of potential escalation and Israeli and U.S. strikes on Yemen targets.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Expert warns of 'general escalation' of fighting if Houthis resume Red Sea campaign

events.title: Inside Tehran: Iranians describe IRGC's brutal rule, poverty — ask Trump to 'stay the course'
events.description: Iranians inside Tehran describe growing IRGC control, economic collapse and bread lines as Trump threatens to hit Iran hard over nuclear talks.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Germany news: 23 million tune in for World Cup win
events.description: The Germany national team's 7-1 win over Curacao drew over 23 million viewers. Meanwhile, German President Steinmeier is kicking off his visit to Indonesia. Follow DW for more.

events.title: ‘Perilous moment’ threatens to reverse years of gains in HIV/AIDS response
events.description: External funding cuts, a backlash against human rights, and chronic under-investment in HIV prevention and community services are threatening to reverse years of hard-won progress in the AIDS response, a UN report warned on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Perilous moment’ threatens to reverse years of gains in HIV/AIDS response

events.title: Missouri skydiving plane crash that killed all 12 aboard is a 'devastating loss,' company says
events.description: Skydive Kansas City says staff remains in shock after a plane crash killed all 12 people aboard, including 11 skydivers and a pilot in Missouri.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Missouri skydiving plane crash that killed all 12 aboard is a 'devastating loss,' company says

events.title: Alleged Malibu serial squatter featured in new docuseries after years of homeowner complaints
events.description: Alleged serial squatter Ellie Mae McNulty is featured in Hulu's new docuseries after reportedly terrorizing Malibu residents by refusing to leave homes.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Alleged Malibu serial squatter featured in new docuseries after years of homeowner complaints

events.title: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
events.description: Somali referee Omar Artan, who was denied entry to the United States to officiate at the World Cup, will still receive his tournament fee from Fifa in full.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee

events.title: Record increase in DRC Ebola cases raises death toll to 181
events.description: The Ebola outbreak, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus strain, has reached 782 confirmed cases.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Record increase in DRC Ebola cases raises death toll to 181

events.title: Geneva police use force as G7 protest escalates into violence
events.description: Demonstrators denounce G7 amid escalating clashes, which lead to damaged buildings and burning cars.

events.title: One Nation branch official defended Hitler Youth and called Aboriginal people ‘stone age’ in racist posts
events.description: Exclusive: Aboriginal Australians and migrants the targets of John Drew, who claims he was once kicked out of Pauline Hanson’s party for being ‘too radical’ – but is now a branch official Warning: This article contains racially offensive language and outdated references Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man who has defended the Hitler Youth organisation and used offensive and racist language about Aboriginal people and migrants is working as a policy development officer for One Nation in Queensland. Guardian Australia can reveal that John Drew, who claims he was kicked out of One Nation more than 20 years ago for being “too radical”, says he has been a party branch official in Brisbane since late last year. This is despite One Nation claiming it has introduced a sophisticated new vetting tool – dubbed Operation Obsidian – that it applies to any person taking up a party role. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 One Nation branch official defended Hitler Youth and called Aboriginal people ‘stone age’ in racist posts

events.title: Inside Ebola country: NPR reports from eastern DR Congo's outbreak zone
events.description: In eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, an Ebola outbreak is reshaping life, with fear spreading faster than information and hospitals turning into places of urgent containment rather than care.

events.title: Brazil : Oliver Tree, “Missed You” singer, killed in helicopter crash
events.description: American alt-pop singer and internet personality Oliver Tree was aboard one of the two helicopters that collided mid-air in Brazil, leaving six dead. The musician was known for hits such as "Miss You" and "Life Goes On." He had over 11 million monthly listeners on Spotify, with his top songs listened to over 700 million times.

events.title: Son of Norway's crown princess sentenced to four years in prison for rape
events.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the son of Norway’s crown princess, was sentenced to four years in prison on Monday after an Oslo court convicted him of two counts of rape and 32 other offences, including violence against a former partner, threats and drug-related crimes.

events.title: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
events.description: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates

events.title: Residents wake up screaming as cops race to rescue them from late-night apartment blaze: video
events.description: Bodycam footage shows Utah police officers breaking down doors and pulling residents from beds during a June 7 apartment fire in St. George.

events.title: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
events.description: The chain was criticised for a "Tank Day" marketing ploy on the anniversary of a military massacre.

events.title: What a UK court’s backing of the Palestine Action ‘terror’ ban means
events.description: Britain's Court of Appeal ruled that the government was right to proscribe Palestine Action - what happens next?
events.ticker_label: 💥 What a UK court’s backing of the Palestine Action ‘terror’ ban means

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: London council seizes social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone first lady
events.description: Fatima Jabbe-Bio kept tenancy in Southwark despite living for much of year at presidential lodge in Freetown A social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone’s first lady has been seized by a London council. Southwark council confirmed it had repossessed the two-bedroom home in Walworth previously occupied by Fatima Jabbe-Bio, whose tenancy was reported by the Times last year. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 London council seizes social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone first lady

events.title: Mourners line Bangkok streets to pay respects to Thailand’s Princess Bha
events.description: Funeral procession travels to palace as people remember royal’s campaigning and work for underprivileged As the sun began to set on the golden spires and gilded finials of Bangkok’s Grand Palace, the gates were open, waiting for the return of a princess. Since December 2022, Princess Bajrakitiyabha had been in hospital, having collapsed while out training her dogs. After nearly four years in a coma, the princess died earlier this week . Continue reading...

Plus the Netherlands and Japan had a tight 90 minutes which ended in a 2-2 draw.

events.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential palace every Thursday since 1977 , demanding the return of children who were disappeared during the country’s 1976-1983 dictatorship. events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.

events.title: Colombian lawmakers seek suspension of Trump foe Gustavo Petro over alleged meddling in upcoming election
events.description: Colombian lawmakers consider suspending President Gustavo Petro from office amid allegations he improperly intervened in the presidential election.
